codeReview.summary: The implementation successfully adds explicit transaction isolation to Database.increment_usage(). The code properly handles both standalone calls and nested transaction contexts by checking the _in_transaction flag. The implementation is well-documented with clear comments explaining the isolation strategy. Comprehensive concurrent testing validates that no race conditions occur under high concurrency (10 threads × 10 increments = 100 updates with no lost updates).

summary: Feature m2-f6-atomic-usage-tracking successfully implements proper transaction isolation for Database.increment_usage(). The implementation wraps the UPDATE statement in an explicit transaction() context manager when called standalone, while avoiding nested transactions when already within a transaction. The worker added 4 comprehensive concurrent tests that verify no race conditions occur under various scenarios: same account concurrent updates, different account concurrent updates, explicit transaction usage, and rollback behavior. All 205 unit tests pass with no regressions. The code is clean, well-documented, and fulfills assertion x-a4 (error handling preserves system stability).
